idea.hook: Want to visit the *closest* star to our Sun?
facts: Proxima Centauri is the nearest star to us, beyond the Sun.
It is 4.2 light-years away, which is roughly 40 TRILLION kilometers (25 trillion miles).
Our fastest spacecraft, Voyager 1, travels at an incredible ~61,000 km/h (~38,000 mph).
idea.payoff: At Voyager 1's speed, it would take OVER 70,000 YEARS to reach Proxima Centauri. Hope you packed snacks!
idea.title: Travel to the Closest Star? 🤯

scenes.text: Want to visit the CLOSEST star to our Sun?
scenes.visual: A vibrant starfield. The Sun glows brightly on the left, a smaller, distant star (Proxima Centauri) twinkles on the right.
Zoom in on the distant star from Scene 1. Text 'Proxima Centauri' appears clearly next to it.

scenes.text: 4.2 light-years away! That's 40 TRILLION km (25 Trillion miles)!
scenes.visual: An infographic line stretches across the screen. 'Earth' appears on the left, 'Proxima Centauri' on the right. Numbers for distance animate along the line.

scenes.text: Our fastest craft, Voyager 1, travels at ~61,000 km/h (~38,000 mph)!
scenes.visual: A stylized Voyager 1 spacecraft flies across the screen from left to right, leaving a faint cosmic trail. A subtle speed dial graphic appears momentarily.

scenes.text: At THAT speed, it would take OVER 70,000 YEARS to get there! (Pack snacks!)
scenes.visual: The Voyager 1 spacecraft from Scene 4 becomes a tiny dot, struggling against the vastness of space. '70,000+ YEARS' appears dramatically large between it and Proxima Centauri. A small, comical snack icon (e.g., a bag of chips) appears briefly.
